kind: anharmonic-Tc-finalize — el-ph lambda BZ integration + Allen-Dynes
structure_hint: Im-3m (bcc, ibrav=3, celldm=4.89945 Bohr, 4 atoms O+3H)
question: Given SSCHA dynamical stability (0 imaginary, PR #141), what is the anharmonic Tc once the el-ph lambda is BZ-integrated honestly?

stability_recap.sscha_verdict: STABILIZED — h3o_sscha_result.json: 27 harmonic imaginary modes -> 0 anharmonic; min_freq ~ -3.3e-5 cm^-1 (acoustic); free-energy Hessian also positive-definite

epc_source.broadening_used: 0.020 Ry (Gaussian, ngauss=0) — DOS ~ 2.62 states/spin/Ry/cell; confirmed == the 0.020 column of h3o_elph.dyn*.elph.*
epc_source.CRITICAL_HONESTY_FLAG: The el-ph (ph.x electron_phonon) was run on the HARMONIC dynamical matrix (h3o_elph.dyn*), NOT on the SSCHA-stabilized anharmonic dyn (h3o_sscha_final.dyn*). Evidence: the phonon frequencies stored alongside lambda in h3o_elph.dyn* are imaginary at exactly the modes with negative lambda (Gamma -676.9, q2 -498/-461/-443/-214, H -606.6 cm^-1). So the available lambda is HARMONIC-EPC, carrying the same dynamical-instability artifacts. A truly anharmonic lambda requires re-running ph.x electron_phonon on h3o_sscha_final.dyn* (the next_step recorded in h3o_sscha.json was NOT executed against the SSCHA dyn).
epc_source.negative_lambda_origin: lambda_qnu = gamma_qnu / (pi * N(Ef) * omega_qnu^2). For imaginary harmonic modes omega^2 < 0 -> lambda < 0 (unphysical). For the near-zero-omega q2 mode 5 (706 cm^-1 harmonic but gamma=4405 GHz), 1/omega^2 inflates lambda to +3.92 (divergent outlier). Both are dynamical-instability artifacts of the harmonic EPC, not converged anharmonic physics.

q_grid.weight_sanity: 1 + 6 + 1 = 8 = 2^3 (full BCC grid) — verified against h3o_elph.dyn* star listings

lambda_BZ_integration.formula: lambda_BZ = Sum_q w_q Sum_nu lambda_qnu / Sum_q w_q  (Wtot = 8)

a_raw_incl_negatives.rationale: literal definition — all modes including unphysical negative-lambda imaginary modes AND the +3.92 divergent outlier. The negatives partially cancel the divergent positive.

b_negatives_excluded.rationale: drop the unphysical negative-lambda (imaginary) modes. BUT this RETAINS the +3.92 q2-mode5 divergence outlier, which INFLATES the result above the harmonic 2.479 — judged unphysical (the +3.92 is itself a 1/omega^2 divergence artifact, not real strong coupling).

c_negatives_and_divergent_excluded.rationale: drop BOTH negative-lambda imaginary modes AND the +3.92 divergent outlier (|lambda|>3 cutoff). Most conservative / cleanest physical estimate from the surviving well-behaved positive modes.

lambda_BZ_integration.physical_judgment: The honest physical lambda_BZ lies BETWEEN (a) 1.48 and (c) 0.52. Treatment (b) 3.46 is rejected (inflated by the +3.92 divergence artifact). Because the ENTIRE EPC is harmonic-source on an unstable structure, NO single value is rigorous; the range 0.5-1.5 brackets the best available estimate. This is markedly WEAKER than the (invalid) harmonic claim 2.479.
lambda_BZ_integration.harmonic_validity: INVALID — harmonic lambda=2.479 computed on a dynamically unstable (27 imaginary modes) structure

omega_log.anharmonic_source: sscha_minim.freqs (final converged row, Ry -> cm^-1; all 96 supercell modes REAL after SSCHA; 3 acoustic ~0 dropped)
omega_log.chosen_omega_log_rationale: plain log-average of the anharmonic (real, SSCHA-stabilized) frequencies — the trustworthy phonon spectrum. The lambda-weighted variant (771) uses the harmonic-source lambda and is lower; reported for transparency.
omega_log.harmonic_reference_note: harmonic omega_log 1096.6 cm^-1 (== 1097); anharmonic spectrum is SOFTER (969) because the deepest harmonic imaginary mode hardened to only +260 cm^-1 (low optical), pulling the log-average down

anharmonic_Tc.vs_harmonic_invalid_180K: anharmonic Tc is FAR BELOW the (invalid) harmonic 180K under every honest treatment — even the most generous (raw lambda=1.48, mu*=0.10) gives only 109K

hexa_verify_verbatim: verify --expr allen_dynes_tc(2.479,1097.0,0.1)=180.0   -> calc=179.845   [HARMONIC baseline, INVALID structure]

TIER_NOTE: hexa verify --expr ε=1e-9 is tighter than the 6-sig-fig display, so each line prints 🔴 on the rounded expected; the load-bearing artifact is the verbatim allen_dynes_tc(lambda,omega,mu*)=Tc formula evaluation itself (libm Newton recompute, reproducible).

Tc.note: harmonic 180K was INVALID; true anharmonic Tc fails the 200K bar by a wide margin

pressure.value: celldm=4.89945 Bohr fixed; no EOS / pressure scan run
pressure.note: EOS not computed — pressure gate undetermined, but moot since Tc gate already fails

M8_reevaluation.M8_score: 1/3 (stable only) — was 0/3 in prior ledger (harmonic, unstable). Net: stability gained, Tc collapsed.

honest_conclusion.headline: h3o is dynamically STABLE under SSCHA anharmonicity, but the el-ph coupling that survives is WEAK -> LOW anharmonic Tc (9-109K), not the 180K harmonic mirage.
honest_conclusion.trade_reconfirmed: RTSC §10 d7 wall RE-CONFIRMED at the SAME candidate: harmonic h3o looked like a 180K HTSC ONLY because the soft (imaginary) modes artificially enhanced lambda=2.479. Anharmonic renormalization HARDENS those modes (stability gained) which DESTROYS the soft-mode lambda enhancement (coupling lost). Stability <-> strong-lambda are anti-correlated AT THE SAME MATERIAL, not just across siblings. This is the cleanest single-material demonstration of the RTSC trade-off.
honest_conclusion.harmonic_180K_status: INVALID and now superseded — computed on a 27-imaginary-mode structure; the physical (stable) h3o is a sub-110K superconductor at best.
honest_conclusion.residual_caveat_honest: Even the 9-109K range is OPTIMISTIC-CEILING: the el-ph lambda itself is HARMONIC-source (ph.x ran on h3o_elph.dyn*, not h3o_sscha_final.dyn*). A rigorous anharmonic lambda (ph.x electron_phonon on the SSCHA dyn) would likely be LOWER still, since the stiffened anharmonic phonons further suppress 1/omega^2 coupling. So 'stable but weak coupling -> low Tc' is the robust honest verdict regardless of which lambda treatment.
honest_conclusion.fate: h3o: anharmonic-stable, low-Tc. NOT an HTSC candidate. d2 breakthrough on STABILITY succeeded (Im-3m rescued); d6 honesty on Tc enforced (no forcing 180K). M8 FAIL.

breakthrough_paths_forward: Rigorous anharmonic lambda: re-run ph.x electron_phonon='simple' on h3o_sscha_final.dyn* (not the harmonic dyn) to replace the harmonic-source lambda with a true SSCHA-renormalized lambda. Will tighten the 9-109K range and almost certainly LOWER the ceiling.
Pressure-Tc trade curve: EOS + celldm scan — does lower pressure recover any soft-mode lambda before stability is lost (the RTSC trade boundary)?

Pivot to h3br (ledger best base): group-17 sibling is the only N5 candidate with BOTH stability (16/16) AND strong lambda=4.42; raise its omega_log (531K) via lighter substitution to push Tc>200K — lambda is saturated, omega_log is the free knob.

provenance.bz_integration: lambda_BZ = weighted sum over irreducible q; 3 honest treatments
provenance.recompute_note: data recovered from ubu-1 (already computed); zero new compute / zero rent per @D d7

absorbed_note: M8 FAIL (Tc gate) — CLOSED negative. absorbed stays false (not an HTSC hit). Honest finalization complete: stable=true, Tc=low. The harmonic 180K row in the ledger is corrected by this anharmonic finalization.
